pipeline.name: membership_base_load
pipeline.description: Load membership base tables from staging Iceberg sources

pre_validations.name: source_member_info_exists
pre_validations.description: Verify member info staging data is available for today
pre_validations.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {source_db}.mrd_mbr_info WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

pre_validations.name: source_product_detail_exists
pre_validations.description: Verify product detail staging data is available for today
pre_validations.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {source_db}.mrd_prd_dtl WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

pre_validations.name: source_household_exists
pre_validations.description: Verify household staging data is available
pre_validations.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {source_db}.mrd_mbrshp_household WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}' OR substring(cast(update_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

steps._comment: ──── WAVE 1: These two temp_iceberg steps run IN PARALLEL (no dependencies) ────
steps.name: create_member_dedup_temp
steps.description: Deduplicate member info staging records by member_id (auto-cleanup temp table)
steps.select_sql: SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY member_id ORDER BY load_timestamp DESC) AS rn FROM {source_db}.mrd_mbr_info WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

steps.name: create_household_dedup_temp
steps.description: Deduplicate household staging records (auto-cleanup temp table)
steps.select_sql: SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY household_id ORDER BY load_timestamp DESC) AS rn FROM {source_db}.mrd_mbrshp_household WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}' OR substring(cast(update_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

steps._comment: ──── WAVE 1 (also parallel): Read external parquet files directly from S3 ────
steps.name: load_external_product_ref
steps.description: Create external table pointing to product reference parquet files on S3

steps._comment: ──── WAVE 1 (also parallel): Create a view as zero-storage virtual temp table ────
steps.name: create_active_members_view
steps.description: Virtual view of active members (zero storage cost, like Spark lazy DataFrame)
steps.view_sql: SELECT member_id, first_name, last_name FROM {source_db}.mrd_mbr_info WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' AND substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

steps._comment: ──── WAVE 2: Merges depend on their respective temp tables (run in PARALLEL) ────
steps.name: merge_member_base
steps.description: Merge deduplicated member records into base table
steps.sql: MERGE INTO {target_db}.member_product_detail_base t USING (SELECT * FROM {target_db}.member_info_dedup_temp WHERE rn = 1) s ON t.member_id = s.member_id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET t.first_name = s.first_name, t.last_name = s.last_name, t.load_timestamp = s.load_timestamp, t.update_timestamp = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (member_id, first_name, last_name, load_timestamp, update_timestamp) VALUES (s.member_id, s.first_name, s.last_name, s.load_timestamp, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

steps.name: merge_household_base
steps.description: Merge household records into base table
steps.sql: MERGE INTO {target_db}.household_members_base t USING (SELECT * FROM {target_db}.household_dedup_temp WHERE rn = 1) s ON t.household_id = s.household_id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET t.member_id = s.member_id, t.relationship = s.relationship, t.load_timestamp = s.load_timestamp, t.update_timestamp = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (household_id, member_id, relationship, load_timestamp, update_timestamp) VALUES (s.household_id, s.member_id, s.relationship, s.load_timestamp, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)

steps._comment: ──── WAVE 3: Optimize steps depend on merges (run in PARALLEL) ────
steps.name: optimize_member_base
steps.description: Compact small files in member base table

steps.name: optimize_household_base
steps.description: Compact small files in household base table

post_validations.name: member_base_row_count
post_validations.description: Verify member base table has today's loaded records
post_validations.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {target_db}.member_product_detail_base WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

post_validations.name: household_base_row_count
post_validations.description: Verify household base table has today's loaded records
post_validations.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {target_db}.household_members_base WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}' OR substring(cast(update_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}'

post_validations.name: member_base_no_duplicates
post_validations.description: Ensure no duplicate member_id in base table
post_validations.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS dup_cnt FROM (SELECT member_id, COUNT(*) AS c FROM {target_db}.member_product_detail_base GROUP BY member_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1)

post_validations.name: staging_vs_base_reconciliation
post_validations.description: Compare staging record count vs base loaded count
post_validations.sql: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {source_db}.mrd_mbr_info WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}') AS staging_cnt, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {target_db}.member_product_detail_base WHERE substring(cast(load_timestamp as varchar), 1, 10) = '{run_date}') AS base_cnt
